ddutil
  • CLI Reference
  • View page source

CLI Reference

Global Options

--version, --license, and --env-file must be placed before the subcommand name. --verbose and --quiet can be placed either before or after the subcommand.

Option

Short

Position

Description

--version

before

Show version and exit

--license

before

Show license information and exit

--verbose

-v

before or after

Enable debug-level output

--quiet

-q

before or after

Suppress all non-error output

--env-file PATH

before

Load a specific .env file (overrides auto-load of .env)

 
ddutil --help
ddutil --version
ddutil --verbose apply
ddutil apply --verbose
ddutil --env-file prod.env apply

Commands

ddutil

DataDog AWS Integration Utility

Manage DataDog integrations for AWS accounts with ease.

Usage

ddutil [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

Options

--version

Show the version and exit.

--license

Show license information and exit

-v, --verbose

Enable verbose output

-q, --quiet

Suppress non-error output

--env-file <env_file>

Path to .env file to load

Default:

'.env'

apply

Create or update DataDog integration for an AWS account.

Checks whether the IAM role and DataDog account integration already exist and creates or updates each as needed.

Example:

ddutil apply –account-id 123456789 –profile aws-prod –dd-account-id YOUR_DD_ACCOUNT

Usage

ddutil apply [OPTIONS]

Options

--account-id <account_id>

AWS Account ID

--profile <profile>

AWS CLI profile name

--role-name <role_name>

IAM role name for DataDog integration

--dd-account-id <dd_account_id>

DataDog account ID

--regions <regions>

Comma-separated list of AWS regions

--services <services>

Comma-separated list of AWS services to monitor

--traces <traces>

Comma-separated list of AWS services for X-Ray tracing

--managed-policies <managed_policies>

Comma-separated list of AWS managed policy ARNs

--policy-actions <policy_actions>

Comma-separated list of additional IAM actions

--partition <partition>

AWS partition (aws, aws-cn, aws-us-gov)

--metric-automute <metric_automute>

Enable/disable automuting (true/false)

--metric-collect-cloudwatch <metric_collect_cloudwatch>

Collect CloudWatch alarms (true/false)

--metric-collect-custom <metric_collect_custom>

Collect custom metrics (true/false)

--metric-collect-metrics <metric_collect_metrics>

Enable metric collection (true/false)

--metric-enable <metric_enable>

Enable metrics collection globally (true/false)

--resource-collect-cspm <resource_collect_cspm>

Enable CSPM collection (true/false)

--resource-collect-extended <resource_collect_extended>

Enable extended resource collection (true/false)

--dry-run

Preview changes without applying

--tags <tags>

Comma-separated IAM role tags in Key=Value format (e.g. Env=prod,Team=ops)

-v, --verbose

Enable verbose output

-q, --quiet

Suppress non-error output

--env-file <env_file>

Path to .env file to load

delete

Delete DataDog integration for an AWS account.

This command deletes the IAM role and removes the account from DataDog.

Example:

ddutil delete –account-id 123456789 –role-name datadog-integration-role –confirm

Usage

ddutil delete [OPTIONS]

Options

--account-id <account_id>

AWS Account ID

--profile <profile>

AWS CLI profile name

--role-name <role_name>

IAM role name for DataDog integration

--confirm

Skip confirmation prompt

-v, --verbose

Enable verbose output

-q, --quiet

Suppress non-error output

--env-file <env_file>

Path to .env file to load

status

Check the status of DataDog integration.

This command validates that the IAM role and DataDog account are configured correctly.

Example:

ddutil status –account-id 123456789

Usage

ddutil status [OPTIONS]

Options

--account-id <account_id>

AWS Account ID

--profile <profile>

AWS CLI profile name

--role-name <role_name>

IAM role name for DataDog integration

--dd-account-id <dd_account_id>

DataDog account ID

-o, --output <output>

Output format

Options:

text | json

-v, --verbose

Enable verbose output

-q, --quiet

Suppress non-error output

--env-file <env_file>

Path to .env file to load

status Sync Values

Status

Meaning

Synced

IAM role and DataDog account exist and config matches .env

Out Of Sync

Both sides exist but one or more settings differ

Partial

Only one of IAM role / DataDog account exists

Not Configured

Neither side exists

Unknown

AWS or DataDog connectivity failed — cannot determine state

status Validated Settings

  • IAM role existence and ARN

  • Attached managed policies vs. expected

  • IAM role tags vs. DD_IAM_TAGS

  • DataDog account registration and External ID

  • Role name consistency between IAM and DataDog

  • AWS partition

  • Monitored regions and service namespaces

  • Metric settings (automute, CloudWatch alarms, custom metrics, enabled)

  • Resource settings (CSPM, extended collection)
